summary: Iteration 140 - Google reCAPTCHA v3 Integration VERIFIED. Backend: 15/15 tests PASSED (100%). Frontend: All 4 protected pages (signup, login, forgot-password, contact) have reCAPTCHA v3 script loaded with visible badge. No CAPTCHA on subscription page (as expected). One minor bug fixed in feedback.py (HTTPException handling).
critical_code_review_comments: Backend: /api/auth/captcha-config correctly returns {enabled: true, provider: 'recaptcha_v3', siteKey: '6LdTGocsAAAAAFHdoM1O6JyeUpSIIayv2GJ0I505'}
Backend: POST /api/auth/register validates reCAPTCHA token - empty/invalid token returns 400
Backend: POST /api/auth/login allows first 2 attempts without CAPTCHA, requires CAPTCHA after 3+ failures
Backend: POST /api/auth/forgot-password verifies reCAPTCHA via X-Captcha-Token header
Backend: POST /api/feedback/contact verifies reCAPTCHA via X-Captcha-Token header
Frontend: useRecaptcha hook correctly loads Google reCAPTCHA v3 script from google.com
Frontend: Login.js uses executeRecaptcha('login') after 2+ failed attempts
Frontend: Contact.js uses executeRecaptcha('contact') and sends X-Captcha-Token header
CSP updated in index.html to allow https://www.google.com and https://www.gstatic.com

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All protected pages have reCAPTCHA v3, no CAPTCHA on subscription page

context_for_next_testing_agent: Google reCAPTCHA v3 integration fully verified. Script loads on signup, login, forgot-password, and contact pages. reCAPTCHA badge is visible in bottom-right corner on those pages. Backend validates tokens via Google API (threshold 0.5). No CAPTCHA on dashboard, subscription, or billing pages. Note: On preview domain, reCAPTCHA tokens may fail verification since domain might not be whitelisted in Google reCAPTCHA admin console - this is expected behavior and not a code issue.

signup_page.backend_validation: PASS - Empty token returns 400 CAPTCHA verification failed

login_page.captcha_after_failures: PASS - CAPTCHA required after 3+ failed attempts

bug_fixed.issue: HTTPException for CAPTCHA failure was being caught by generic except block and re-raised as 500
bug_fixed.fix: Added 'except HTTPException: raise' before generic exception handler
